scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Urgency level boundary gap fixed — 3-14 day range now correctly labeled Urgent with clarifying note. Timezone default documented in workflow step 2. All five operations covered.

reliability.note: Timezone validation step added. Urgency levels table corrected. Error handling documented. Minor: no explicit handling for past-deadline inputs.

performance_context.note: References directory present with journal_deadlines.md, revision_checklist.md, and task_templates.json; good progressive disclosure.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear with timezone validation step. Urgency table corrected. Stress-case rules and response template present.

human_usability.note: Description is highly discoverable for academic researchers; forgiveness good — most fields are optional.

security.note: No credentials required; input validation present; no sensitive data exposure risk.

maintainability.note: Clean structure with references/ directory; task templates in separate JSON file enables easy updates. Urgency boundary now consistent.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; progressive disclosure via references/; composability moderate — no structured output schema for downstream tools.

key_strengths: Urgency level boundary gap fixed — 3-14 day range now correctly labeled Urgent with clarifying note, eliminating the v1 inconsistency
Timezone default now documented in workflow step 2 with explicit note and guidance to specify local timezone
Three-tier urgency system (standard/urgent/emergency) with distinct task strategies is well-calibrated for real academic workflows
References directory with task_templates.json and revision_checklist.md enables good progressive disclosure and easy maintenance

recommendations.title: No handling for past-deadline inputs
recommendations.problem: The skill has no documented behavior for when a user enters a deadline that has already passed, which could produce confusing urgency calculations.
recommendations.root_cause: The urgency level table only covers future deadlines; past deadlines are not addressed.
recommendations.fix: Add a fourth urgency state 'Overdue' for past-deadline inputs, with guidance on requesting an extension or archiving the manuscript.

recommendations.title: Default timezone Asia/Shanghai may be incorrect for most international users
recommendations.problem: The default timezone is Asia/Shanghai, which may cause deadline miscalculations for users in other regions who do not specify --timezone.
recommendations.root_cause: The --timezone parameter defaults to Asia/Shanghai without explanation of why this was chosen.
recommendations.fix: Consider changing the default timezone to UTC and documenting that users should specify their local timezone via --timezone to avoid deadline calculation errors.
